pattern: Flood-fill (DFS or BFS) each unvisited land cell, marking the entire connected region as visited; each flood-fill call counts as one island.
hints: Scan cell by cell; when you land on an unvisited '1', you've found a new island — now you must claim every land cell reachable from it so you don't count them again.
A recursive DFS (or iterative BFS/stack) from the starting cell, replacing '1' with '0' (or a visited marker) as it goes, claims the entire connected region in one pass.
Outer loop increments the island counter once per flood-fill invocation; the flood-fill itself explores in all four cardinal directions and stops at '0' or the grid boundary.

constraints.note: m rows, n cols, each in 1..300

edge_inputs.description: all cells are water — zero islands
all cells are land — one big island
diagonal land cells are NOT connected (cardinal-only adjacency)
single row, alternating land/water — every land cell is its own island
single column, three separated land cells
large contiguous land mass with one interior water cell — still one island
two islands sharing the same column but separated by a water row

stress.description: 300x300 checkerboard — alternating land/water, many isolated islands
stress.note: each '1' is surrounded by '0's so island count equals the number of '1' cells
